author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke ix. 57, etc., where the case of the three persons is treated of, of whom one said, ‘I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest,’ and was disallowed: another did not dare to offer himself, and was aroused; the third wished to delay, and was blamed.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke ix. 57, etc., where the case of the three persons is treated of, of whom one said, ‘I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest,’ and was disallowed: another did not dare to offer himself, and was aroused; the third wished to delay, and was blamed.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ye ear to that which the Lord hath given me to speak on the lesson of the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have read, that the Lord Jesus acted differently, when one man offered himself to follow Him, and was disallowed; another did not dare this, and was aroused; a third put off, and was blamed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the words, “Lord, I will follow Thee whithersoever Thou goest,” what is so prompt, what so active, what so ready, and what so fitly disposed to so great a good, as this “following the Lord whithersoever He should go”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wonderest at this, saying, “How is this, that one so ready found no favour with the Good Master and Lord Jesus Christ, though He was inviting disciples to give them the kingdom of Heaven?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But inasmuch as He was such a Master as could see beforehand things to come, we understand, Brethren, that this man, if he had followed Christ, would have been sure to “seek his own things, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath said Himself, “Not every one that saith unto Me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And of such was this man, nor did he know himself so well as the Physician knew him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if he saw himself to be a dissembler now, if he had known himself at this time to be full of duplicity and guile, then he did not know with Whom he was speaking.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He it is of whom the Evangelist says, “He had no need that any one should testify to Him of man, for He Himself knew what was in man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did He answer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man hath not where to lay His Head.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But where hath He not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In thy faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in thy heart foxes have holes, thou art full of guile; in thy heart birds of the air have nests; thou art lifted up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Full of guile and self-elation as thou art, thou shalt not follow Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can a guileful man follow Simplicity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then forthwith to another who was silent, and said nothing, and promised nothing, He saith, “Follow Me!”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As much evil as He saw in the other, so much good saw He in this man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Follow Me,” Thou sayest to one who hath no wish for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, here is a man quite ready, “I will follow Thee whithersoever Thou goest;” and yet Thou sayest to another who hath no such wish, “Follow thou Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The first,” saith He, “I decline, because I see in him holes, I see nests.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But then why dost Thou press this other, whom Thou dost challenge to follow Thee, and he makes excuses?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, Thou dost even force him, and he doth not come; Thou dost exhort him, and he doth not follow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what doth he say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘I will go first to bury my father.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The faith of his heart showed itself to the Lord; but his dutiful affection made him delay.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord Christ when He is preparing men for the Gospel, will have no excuse from this carnal and temporal affection interfere.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is true that both the law of God prescribes these duties, and the Lord Himself reproves the Jews, because they destroyed this very commandment of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Apostle Paul has in his Epistle laid it down, and said, “This is the first commandment with promise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Honour thy father and thy mother.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God of a surety spake it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This young man then wished to obey God, and to bury his father; but it is place, and time, and circumstance, which is in this case to give way to place, and time, and circumstance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A father must be honoured, but God must be obeyed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that begat us must be loved, but He that created us must be preferred.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am calling thee,” saith He, “to My Gospel; I have need of thee for another work: this is a greater work than that which thou wishest to be doing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Let the dead bury their dead.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy father is dead: there are other dead men to bury the dead.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are the dead who bury the dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Can a dead man be buried by dead men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can they lay him out, if they are dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can they carry him, if they are dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How can they bewail him, if they are dead?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet they do lay him out, and carry, and bewail him, and they are dead; because they are unbelievers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which is written in the Song of Songs is a lesson to us, when the Church says, “Set in order love in me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Set in order love in me”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Make the proper degrees, and render to each what is his due.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not put what should come before, below that which should come after it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Love your parents, but prefer God to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark the mother of the Maccabees, “‘My sons, I know not how ye appeared in my womb.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Conceive you I could, give you birth I could; but ‘form you I could not:’ hear Him therefore, prefer Him to me: trouble not yourselves, that I must remain here without you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus she commanded them, and they followed her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What this mother taught her children, did the Lord Jesus Christ teach him to whom He said, “Follow Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See now how another disciple presented himself, to whom no one said anything: he said, “Lord, I will follow Thee, but I will first go to bid them farewell which are at my house.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suppose this is his meaning, “Let me tell my friends, lest haply they seek me as usual.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said, “No man putting his hand on the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The East calls thee, and thou art looking toward the west.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this lesson we learn this, that the Lord chooses whom He will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But He chooses them, as the Apostle says, both according to His Own grace, and according to their righteousness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For such are the words of the Apostle; “Attend,” he says, “to what Elias saith: Lord, they have killed Thy Prophets, they have overthrown Thine altars, and I am left alone, and they seek my life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what saith the answer of God to him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have reserved to Myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee before Baal.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou thinkest that thou art the only servant who is working faithfully: there are others too who fear Me, and they not few.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I have “seven thousand” there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then he added, “Even so then at this present time also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For some Jews believed, though the most were reprobate; like him who carried holes for foxes in his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Even so then,” saith he, “at this present time also, there is a remnant saved through the election of grace:” that is, there is the same Christ even now, as then, who also then said to that Elias, “I have reserved to Myself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “I have reserved to Myself”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have chosen them, because I saw their hearts that they trusted in Me, and not in themselves, nor in Baal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are not changed, they are as they were made by Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thou who art speaking, except thou hadst placed thy trust in Me, where wouldest thou be?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Except thou wert replenished by My grace, wouldest not thou too be bowing the knee before Baal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But thou art replenished by My grace; because thou hast not put thy trust at all in thine own strength, but wholly in My grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not therefore glory in this, as to suppose thou hast no fellow-servants in thy service; there are others whom I have chosen, as I have chosen thee, those, namely, who put their trust in Me; as the Apostle says, “Even now also a remnant is saved through the election of grace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Beware, O Christian, beware of pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For though thou art a follower of the saints, ascribe it always wholly to grace; for that there should be any “remnant” in thee, the grace of God hath brought to pass, not thine own deserts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Prophet Isaiah again having this remnant in view, had said already, “Except the Lord of Hosts had left us a seed, we should have become as Sodom, and should have been like unto Gomorrah.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “So then,” says the Apostle, “at this present time also a remnant is saved through the election of grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if by grace,” says he, “then is it no more of works” (that is, “be now no more lifted up upon thine own deserts”); “otherwise grace is no more grace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou dost build on thine own work; then is a reward rendered unto thee, not grace freely bestowed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if it be grace, it is gratuitously given.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask thee then, O sinner, “Dost thou believe in Christ?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou sayest, “I do believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What dost thou believe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That all thy sins may be forgiven thee freely through Him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then hast thou what thou hast believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O grace gratuitously given!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thou, righteous man, what dost thou believe, that thou canst not keep thy righteousness without God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That thou art righteous then, impute it wholly to His mercy; but that thou art a sinner, ascribe it to thine own iniquity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be thou thine own accuser, and He will be thy gracious Deliverer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every crime, wickedness, or sin comes of our own negligence, and all virtue and holiness come of God’s gracious goodness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let us turn to the Lord.”
